title: Intelligence vs. Wisdom in Islam (And What Iblis Teaches Us) | Snapshots with Imam Tom Facchine
description:
It’s easy to assume that more knowledge automatically makes someone closer to God, but intelligence and wisdom were never the same thing. Some of the most knowledgeable people in Islamic studies aren't practicing Muslims at all: they’re fluent in the language, expert in the texts, and still missing the point entirely. That's exactly what happens when knowledge gets separated from action.

In this Snapshot, Imam Tom Facchine unpacks why intelligence and wisdom are not the same, and why the Qur'an warns that knowledge without action can become a source of harm rather than benefit.

This episode covers:
 — Why knowledge without action breeds hypocrisy
 — The difference between mastering a text and living by it
 — What Iblis's story teaches us about intelligence versus guidance
 — Why the Companions learned the Qur'an differently than we do today

#intelligence #wisdom #islam #knowledge

title: The “Approval” Idol | Signs of the Hour Ep. 12 | Dr. Omar Suleiman
description:
Will Islam one day look unrecognizable, the way other major religions have? The Prophet ﷺ left his ummah a stark warning: don't follow previous nations into a lizard's hole. It's a call to hold firm to your faith, even when the crowd moves elsewhere. 

In this episode, Dr. Omar Suleiman talks about the 10 patterns that led other religions astray and what every Muslim needs to watch for today. 

0:00 - You will follow the ways of those before you 
1:05 - What is the lizard’s hole?
2:40 - Our closeness to Jews and Christians 
5:31 - Patterns of corruption 
8:22 - How the community becomes divided 
10:41 - The 7 steps of Shaytan 
13:06 - The origins of the word “hypocrisy”
14:23 - 10 patterns of how communities lose their religion 

#signsofthehour #dayofjudgment #endtimes #lecture #omarsuleiman

title: A Fool with a Platform | Dr. Omar Suleiman
description:
Notice how the loudest people in the audience are often the ones who never actually studied anything?

The Prophet ﷺ warned about this exactly, a time when the lowly are raised, the noble are put down, and the people you end up trusting are the ones who sound the most like what you already wanted to believe.

Full episode is up now: https://yqn.io/e1ed

title: ‘The Du’a That Changed My Entire Life’ | Iman Cave with Sh. Abdullah Oduro
description:
Marriage season is here, and everyone wants to know the secret.
This isn't a blueprint for getting married in four weeks.

Rather, it's a reminder that when you stop trying to force every outcome and sincerely ask Allah for what's best, your heart changes before your circumstances do.

The du'a in this story is one worth remembering. Watch Sami Hamdi’s full story here: https://yqn.io/udeh

title: Murarah (ra) and Hilal (ra): The Companions Who Waited Too Long | The Firsts | Dr. Omar Suleiman
description:
The story of Hilal and Murarah (ra) is one we should emulate.

They made a mistake, and the Prophet ﷺ gave them specific instructions for their tawbah. Instead of being arrogant and refusing to take responsibility, they owned their mistake and made repentance. 

In this episode, Dr. Omar Suleiman details their little-known experience. Reflect on how you can apply these lessons in your own life.

0:00 - The forgotten two from Tabuk 
2:31 - Verses 118-119 of Surah Tawbah 
3:42 - Why they’re an example for us
4:43 - Murarah ibn Rabi’a (ra)'s background
5:48 - Hilal ibn Umayyah (ra)'s background 
9:12 - Their procrastination during Tabuk 
13:15 - The sincerity of their repentance 
16:25 - How Allah described their inner pain 
19:58 - Forgiveness and lasting lessons

#thefirsts #tabuk #repentance #kabibnmalik #lecture #omarsuleiman

title: Why Small Good Deeds Matter
description:
You fast a few hours. He gives eternity. You give a dollar. He turns it into mountains. You ease one difficulty. He eases yours in this life and the next. Allah is Al-Muhsin. He does more than what you deserve. Always.

Watch "Does Allah Love Me?" with Dr. Omar Suleiman: http://yqn.io/rn3q

description:

But some of his jokes went too far. How did the Prophet ﷺ respond?

title: Ask Allah To Change YOU Instead | LATEST Khutbah by Dr. Omar Suleiman
description:
The next time you raise your hands, don’t stop asking Allah to change your circumstances. But before you ask Him to change your world, ask Him to change your heart. Sometimes the greatest answer to a du’a is not that Allah changes what surrounds you, but that He changes who you become within it. 

Memorize this du’a that the Prophet ﷺ would make.

“O Allah grant me Your love and the love of those whose love will benefit me with You. O Allah, whatever you have provided me of that which I love, then make it strength for me for that which You love. O Allah, and what you have kept from me of that which I love, then make it for me a period of rest in that which You love.” 

اللَّهُمَّ ارْزُقْنِي حُبَّكَ وَحُبَّ مَنْ يَنْفَعُنِي حُبُّهُ عِنْدَكَ اللَّهُمَّ مَا رَزَقْتَنِي مِمَّا أُحِبُّ فَاجْعَلْهُ قُوَّةً لِي فِيمَا تُحِبُّ 
اللَّهُمَّ وَمَا زَوَيْتَ عَنِّي مِمَّا أُحِبُّ فَاجْعَلْهُ لِي فَرَاغًا فِيمَا تُحِبُّ 

Allahumma’rzuqni hubbak wa hubba yanfa‘uni hubbuhu indak. Allahumma ma razaqtani mimma uhibbu faj‘alhu quwwatan li fi ma tuhibbu. Allahumma wa ma zawayta anni mimma uhibbu faj‘alhu li faraghan fi ma tuhibbu.  

[Source: Tirmidhi #3491, hasan]

0:00 - Opening Praise
2:19 - Change You, Not Decree
3:49 - Du'a Is More Than A Wish List
5:29 - Rare Inner Du'as
7:16 - Ya Muqallib al-qulub
9:51 - Ya Musarrif al-qulub
11:48 - Contentment with Qadr
17:17 - Approval from Others
21:48 - Loving What Allah Loves
23:00 - Closing Duas

#prophetic #dua #khutbah #malaysia #omarsuleiman

title: The Most Dangerous Kind of Muslim Debate | Snapshots with Imam Tom Facchine
description:
Disagreement within the ummah is inevitable, but how do we disagree without allowing our differences to become a source of division and hostility?

In this Snapshot, Imam Tom Facchine explains the deeper meaning of naseeha: not simply advice but sincere loyalty and concern for each other. He explores how Muslims can maintain the principles of Islam while preserving the truth, justice, and brotherhood, and outlines the four levels of blameworthy sectarianism that every Muslim should be able to recognize.  

This episode covers: 
— The meaning of naseeha and why sincere advice is essential in Islam
— The difference between legitimate disagreement and destructive sectarianism
— The four levels of blameworthy sectarianism

title: 4 Voices the Prophet ﷺ Told Us Not to Follow | Signs of the Hour Ep. 11 | Dr. Omar Suleiman
description:
A time is coming when liars will be seen as trustworthy, and the honest will be branded as deceivers. The Prophet ﷺ warned us about becoming one of these people—including the person who has an opinion on everything, yet contributes nothing.

In this lecture, Dr. Omar Suleiman dives deep into this powerful warning and what it means for how we carry ourselves, speak, and show up for the people around us.

00:00 - Many Dajjals Before the Dajjal
02:05 - Ar-Ruwaybidah: Degenerate Public Commentators

title: Abu Mihjan (ra): The Sahabi With A Substance Use Disorder | The Firsts | Dr. Omar Suleiman
description:
He was fierce on the battlefield, yet fighting a quieter battle within himself.

Abu Mihjan (ra) had a weakness, but it never stopped his courage. He owned his struggle and still showed up for his community, time and again. What finally became his turning point?

title: Praying Janazah 30 Years Later | Dr. Omar Suleiman
description:
"Not all of us are able to find bones."

Witnessing something like this changes you… and it should.

These graves needed the world to step up in 1995, and watched it stay silent instead. That's exactly why we can't stay silent now, for anyone being oppressed, anywhere.

title: What Happens When You Say “Astaghfirullah”
description:
You’re not just asking to be pardoned. You’re asking to be guarded. From shame in this life. From exposure in the next. From becoming the worst version of yourself. 

Al-Ghaffar doesn’t close the door because you knocked twice. He opens it every time. 

Watch "Will Allah Forgive Me?": http://yqn.io/eyzw

description:
